document: State Bank of India - Standalone Financials, Annual Report 2023-24
description: Golden evaluation set for RAG pipelines indexing this document. Each entry includes the query, the ground-truth answer, the page(s) that should be retrieved, and keyword anchors useful for checking retrieval recall (Recall@k) independent of exact chunk boundaries.

entries.query: What was SBI's total balance sheet size as at 31st March 2024?
entries.expected_answer: Total assets/liabilities stood at ₹6,179,693.94 crore (in thousands: 6179693,94,50) as at 31st March 2024, up from ₹5,516,978.53 crore in the previous year.

entries.query: What was SBI's net profit for FY2023-24?
entries.expected_answer: Net Profit for the year ended 31st March 2024 was ₹61,076.62 crore, compared to ₹50,232.45 crore in the previous year.

entries.query: What is SBI's basic and diluted earnings per share for FY24?
entries.expected_answer: Basic EPS was ₹68.44 and Diluted EPS was also ₹68.44 (face value ₹1 per share), compared to ₹56.29 in the previous year.

entries.query: What was the Capital to Risk Weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) as at 31st March 2024?
entries.expected_answer: CRAR was 14.28% as at 31st March 2024, down from 14.68% in the previous year. Tier 1 capital ratio was 11.93% and Tier 2 was 2.35%.
expected_chunk_keywords: Capital to Risk Weighted Assets Ratio

entries.query: What was the Gross NPA ratio and Net NPA ratio for SBI in FY24?
entries.expected_answer: Gross NPA to Gross Advances was 2.24% (down from 2.78%) and Net NPA to Net Advances was 0.57% (down from 0.67%) as at 31st March 2024.

entries.query: What was total deposits and total advances for SBI as at 31st March 2024?
entries.expected_answer: Total Deposits were ₹4,916,076.77 crore and Total Advances were ₹3,703,970.85 crore as at 31st March 2024.

entries.query: How did SBI's Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) trend across the four quarters of FY24, and what was the consolidated Group LCR at year end?
entries.expected_answer: Standalone quarterly LCR for FY24 was: Q1 (Jun'23) 146.92%, Q2 (Sep'23) 145.50%, Q3 (Dec'23) 137.79%, Q4 (Mar'24) 129.02% — a declining trend through the year. The consolidated SBI Group LCR as at 31st March 2024 was 130.62%.

entries.query: What was SBI's Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) standalone as at 31st March 2024?
entries.expected_answer: Standalone NSFR was 113.87% as at 31st March 2024, with Total ASF of ₹43,75,305 crore and Total RSF of ₹38,42,230 crore.

entries.query: What dividend did SBI declare for FY2023-24?
entries.expected_answer: The Central Board declared a dividend of ₹13.70 per share (1370%) for the year ended 31st March 2024.
expected_chunk_keywords: Central Board has declared a dividend

entries.query: How many fraud cases did SBI report during FY24 and what amount was involved?
entries.expected_answer: SBI reported 1,586 frauds amounting to ₹3,406.87 crore during FY24, fully provided for. Additionally, 13,158 digital payment frauds amounting to ₹88.91 crore were reported separately.

entries.query: Who was the Chairman of State Bank of India as per this annual report?
entries.expected_answer: Shri Dinesh Kumar Khara was the Chairman of State Bank of India as per the FY2023-24 annual report.

entries.query: Who were the Managing Directors of SBI during FY2023-24, and were there any changes during the year?
entries.expected_answer: The Managing Directors were: Shri Challa Sreenivasulu Setty (International Banking, Global Markets & Technology), Shri Ashwini Kumar Tewari (Corporate Banking & Subsidiaries), Shri Alok Kumar Choudhary (Risk, Compliance & SARG), and Shri Vinay M. Tonse (Retail Business & Operations, w.e.f. 21st November 2023). Shri Swaminathan Janakiraman served as MD (Corporate Banking & Subsidiaries) until 25th June 2023, after which Shri Ashwini Kumar Tewari took over that portfolio.

entries.query: What penalties did RBI impose on SBI during FY2023-24?
entries.expected_answer: RBI imposed penalties of ₹1 crore for non-compliance on 'Loans and Advances - Statutory and Other Restrictions' and ₹0.30 crore for non-compliance with intra-group transaction guidelines (order dated 25th Sept 2023); and ₹1 crore each for contravention of Section 19(2) and Section 26A(2) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (order dated 26th Feb 2024). Total penalties: ₹3.30 crore.

entries.query: What was the Provision Coverage Ratio (PCR) for SBI as at 31st March 2024?
entries.expected_answer: PCR excluding AUCA was 75.02% and PCR including AUCA was 91.89% as at 31st March 2024.

entries.query: What was the total amount of investments held by SBI as at 31st March 2024, and how is it split between India and outside India?
entries.expected_answer: Total Investments were ₹1,671,339.66 crore, comprising ₹1,605,083.61 crore in India and ₹66,256.05 crore outside India.

entries.query: How much provision did SBI make as exceptional items in FY24 and what were they for?
entries.expected_answer: SBI recognised ₹7,100 crore as exceptional items: ₹5,400 crore for estimated liability from moving to a uniform 50% pension rate for all pensioners, and ₹1,700 crore for ex-gratia benefit and neutralisation of Dearness Relief to pre-November 2002 retirees and family pensioners. This appears both in Schedule 16 (operating expenses) and in the segment reporting result adjustment.

entries.query: What was SBI's Return on Assets (ROA) for FY2023-24?
entries.expected_answer: Return on Assets (on net-asset basis) was 1.36% in FY24, up from 0.96% in the previous year.

entries.query: How many complaints did SBI receive from customers in FY24 and how many were pending at year end?
entries.expected_answer: SBI received 32,33,561 complaints during FY24 (down 16.30% from previous year), and 1,31,423 complaints were pending at year end.
expected_chunk_keywords: Number of complaints received during the year
pending at the end of the year

entries.query: What is the par/face value of SBI's equity shares?
entries.expected_answer: The face value (nominal value) of SBI's equity shares is ₹1 per share.

entries.query: Compare SBI's net profit contribution from domestic vs. foreign operations in FY24.
entries.expected_answer: Of the total net profit of ₹61,076.62 crore in FY24, domestic operations contributed ₹49,032.85 crore and foreign operations contributed ₹12,043.77 crore (per the AS-17 secondary/geographic segment disclosure).

entries.query: What were the four primary business segments used by SBI for segment reporting in FY24?
entries.expected_answer: Treasury, Corporate/Wholesale Banking, Retail Banking (with Digital Banking as a sub-segment), and Other Banking Business.

entries.query: What statutory reserve transfer did SBI make in FY24?
entries.expected_answer: SBI transferred ₹18,322.98 crore to Statutory Reserve in FY24 (closing balance ₹118,960.74 crore), compared to ₹15,069.74 crore transferred in the previous year.

entries.query: What was SBI's exposure to the real estate sector as at 31st March 2024?
entries.expected_answer: Total exposure to the Real Estate Sector was ₹947,857.57 crore, comprising ₹714,143.23 crore in residential mortgages, ₹71,840.90 crore in commercial real estate, and ₹161,873.44 crore in indirect exposure to NHB/HFCs.
expected_chunk_keywords: Total Exposure to Real Estate Sector

entries.query: Which audit firms jointly audited SBI's standalone financial statements for FY24?
entries.expected_answer: Twelve joint statutory auditors signed the report, including K C Mehta & Co LLP, M C Bhandari & Co., V Singhi & Associates, Suri & Co., Talati & Talati LLP, Ravi Rajan & Co. LLP, Gokhale & Sathe, M K Aggarwal & Co., J L N U S & Co., Vinod Kumar & Associates, R G N Price & Co., and Rama K Gupta & Co.

entries.query: What were the key audit matters identified by auditors for SBI's FY24 standalone audit?
entries.expected_answer: Four key audit matters: (1) Classification of Advances, Income Recognition, and NPA Provisioning; (2) Classification and Valuation of Investments and NPI provisioning; (3) Assessment of Provisions and Contingent Liabilities for litigations including tax matters; (4) Valuation of employees' defined benefit obligations.
